Home › Visa Checker › Argentine passport
Passport guide · Argentine

Argentine passport: 134 visa-free destinations

Holders of a Argentina passport have easy entry to 134 countries — 106 visa-free and 28 with visa on arrival. Below is the full, sourced list plus e-visa and visa-required countries.

106visa-free
28visa on arrival
44e-visa
20visa required

Visa-free destinations

Countries a Argentine passport holder can enter without arranging any visa in advance. Day-limit shown where available.

  • Andorra✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa-free entry for up to 90 days; enter via Spain or France under Schengen Visa Waiver Agreement.
    Official source ↗
  • Angola✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina nationals are visa-free for up to 30 days per entry, maximum 90 days per calendar year under Presidential Decree 189/23.
    Official source ↗
  • Antigua and Barbuda✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    Argentina passport holders are exempt from visa requirements and can stay for up to 180 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Armenia✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    Argentine citizens may enter Armenia visa-free and stay up to 180 days per calendar year under bilateral agreement.
    Official source ↗
  • Austria✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina passport holders can enter Austria visa-free for tourism up to 90 days within 180 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Bahamas✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina passport holders do not require a visa for tourism; maximum stay is 3 months with valid passport.
    Official source ↗
  • Barbados✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens with ordinary passports are visa-free for up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Belarus✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa-free entry for up to 90 consecutive days from first entry within a calendar year.
    Official source ↗
  • Belgium✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina listed in Annex II of EU Regulation 2018/1806; visa-free for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Belize✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens do not require a visa for tourist visits to Belize for stays up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Bolivia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can visit Bolivia visa-free for up to 90 days per year as tourists.
    Official source ↗
  • Bosnia and Herzegovina✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina passport holders do not require a visa for short-term tourist stays of up to 90 days in Bosnia and Herzegovina.
    Official source ↗
  • Botswana✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens are visa-exempt for tourism; stay limited to 90 days within a 365-day period. Passport must be valid for 6 months.
    Official source ↗
  • Brazil✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter Brazil visa-free for tourism for up to 90 days under the Mercosur visa waiver agreement.
    Official source ↗
  • Bulgaria✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine nationals are visa-exempt for tourist stays of up to 90 days within any 180-day period under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II.
    Official source ↗
  • Chile✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens enter visa-free and receive a Tourist Card upon arrival; maximum 90-day stay permitted.
    Official source ↗
  • China✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens can enter China visa-free for up to 30 days for tourism and other permitted purposes until December 31, 2026.
    Official source ↗
  • Colombia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-exempt for tourism; granted 90-day stay on arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Costa Rica✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    Argentina is Group 1; Argentine citizens receive visa-free entry for up to 180 days upon arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Croatia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine nationals are exempt from visa requirements for short stays in the Schengen area, including Croatia; maximum 90 days in any 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Cyprus✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens may enter Cyprus for up to 90 days for tourism without a visa.
    Official source ↗
  • Czech Republic✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens may enter Czech Republic visa-free for up to 90 days under bilateral agreement; registration with Foreign Police within 3 days required.
    Official source ↗
  • Denmark✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa-free entry as a Schengen country; 90 days within any 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Dominica✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens may enter visa-free for up to 3 months with valid passport and return ticket.
    Official source ↗
  • Dominican Republic✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens do not need a visa for tourism; valid passport with 6+ months validity required.
    Official source ↗
  • Ecuador✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    Argentine citizens can enter Ecuador visa-free with valid passport (6+ months validity) or DNI for up to 180 days per calendar year.
    Official source ↗
  • El Salvador✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-free for up to 90 days; a $12 USD tourist card is required upon arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Estonia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens may stay visa-free in Estonia for up to 90 days within any 180-day period as part of the Schengen Area.
    Official source ↗
  • Fiji✓
    Visa-free · 120 days
    Argentina passport holders are visa-exempt and receive a visitor permit for up to 4 months upon arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Finland✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa-free access to Schengen Zone for tourist stays up to 90 days within 180 days.
    Official source ↗
  • France✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa-free entry for tourist stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period within the Schengen area.
    Official source ↗
  • Georgia✓
    Visa-free · 365 days
    Argentine citizens may enter Georgia visa-free and stay for up to one year with a valid passport.
    Official source ↗
  • Germany✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-free for tourist entry to Germany with a maximum stay of 90 days within any 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Greece✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-free in Greece; can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day Schengen period.
    Official source ↗
  • Grenada✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina nationals can enter Grenada visa-free for up to 30 days as a tourist.
    Official source ↗
  • Guatemala✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-exempt and can enter Guatemala for tourism for up to 90 days with a valid passport.
    Official source ↗
  • Guyana✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine passport holders can enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days with a valid passport.
    Official source ↗
  • Haiti✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa-free for up to 90 days; passport must be valid for 6+ months; $10 USD tourist fee required on arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Honduras✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina passport holders do not require a visa for tourism stays up to 90 days in Honduras.
    Official source ↗
  • Hong Kong✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter Hong Kong visa-free for tourism for up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Hungary✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens are visa-free under the Schengen framework; 90 days per 180-day period allowed.
    Official source ↗
  • Iceland✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina is visa-exempt under Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 Annex II for up to 90 days in any 180-day period within the Schengen area.
    Official source ↗
  • Ireland✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-free for tourism to Ireland for up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Italy✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens do not need a visa for Italy; visa-free entry for up to 90 days within a 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Jamaica✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina passport holders can enter Jamaica visa-free for up to 30 days as tourists.
    Official source ↗
  • Japan✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-exempt; 90-day visa-free entry permitted for short-term tourism.
    Official source ↗
  • Kazakhstan✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina citizens may enter Kazakhstan visa-free for up to 30 days; passport must be valid for 6 months beyond stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Kiribati✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina passport holders are visa-exempt for tourism stays up to 30 days under the Immigration Entry and Visa Exemption Order 2023.
    Official source ↗
  • Kyrgyzstan✓
    Visa-free · 60 days
    Argentine citizens enter visa-free for up to 60 days; e-visa option available for tourism purposes.
    Official source ↗
  • Latvia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter Latvia without a visa and stay for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Liechtenstein✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina passport holders do not require a visa for Schengen Zone travel, including Liechtenstein, for stays up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Lithuania✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-free for Schengen including Lithuania for up to 90 days per 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Luxembourg✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens enjoy visa-free entry to Luxembourg (Schengen member) for up to 90 days within any 180-day period for tourist purposes.
    Official source ↗
  • Macao✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-exempt; maximum stay of 90 days for tourism.
    Official source ↗
  • Malaysia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa-free entry for 90 days as a tourist; passport must be valid for 6+ months.
    Official source ↗
  • Malta✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter Malta visa-free and stay for up to 90 days within any 180-day period as part of Schengen Area regulations.
    Official source ↗
  • Mauritius✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    Argentine nationals may enter Mauritius visa-free for up to 180 days per calendar year for tourism purposes.
    Official source ↗
  • Mexico✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    Argentine citizens do not require a visa for tourist entry to Mexico and may stay up to 180 days as a visitor.
    Official source ↗
  • Micronesia✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Visa-free entry for tourists; valid passport required with 120+ days validity, proof of onward travel, and sufficient funds.
    Official source ↗
  • Moldova✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter visa-free for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Monaco✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine nationals are visa-exempt from Schengen (which Monaco follows) for up to 90 days with a valid passport.
    Official source ↗
  • Mongolia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter visa-free for up to 90 days under the February 19, 2018 bilateral agreement.
    Official source ↗
  • Montenegro✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Nationals of Argentina may enter, pass through the territory of and stay in Montenegro up to 90 days with a valid travel document, without a visa.
    Official source ↗
  • Morocco✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens enter visa-free for up to 90 days; passport must be valid 6+ months.
    Official source ↗
  • Netherlands✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens do not need a visa to visit the Netherlands; visa-free entry for up to 90 days within 180 days under Schengen rules.
    Official source ↗
  • Nicaragua✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-free for up to 90 days under Decreto 002-2026; $10 tourist card required on arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • North Macedonia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter visa-free for up to 90 days within a 180-day period for tourism.
    Official source ↗
  • Norway✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens do not need a visa to visit Norway and can stay visa-free for up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Oman✓
    Visa-free · 14 days
    Argentine passport holders exempt from entry visas for stays up to 14 days; must provide return ticket, hotel reservation, health insurance, and proof of funds.
    Official source ↗
  • Panama✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter Panama visa-free and stay up to 90 days as tourists.
    Official source ↗
  • Paraguay✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine MERCOSUR citizens enter visa-free for up to 90 days with valid passport or national ID.
    Official source ↗
  • Peru✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-free for tourism in Peru for stays up to 90 days; no advance visa application required.
    Official source ↗
  • Philippines✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Visa-free entry for 30 days under Executive Order 408; requires passport valid 6+ months and return/onward ticket.
    Official source ↗
  • Poland✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can stay visa-free for 90 days within a 180-day period in Poland.
    Official source ↗
  • Portugal✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens are visa-exempt for short stays in the Schengen area; up to 90 days in a 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Qatar✓
    Visa-free
    Reciprocal visa exemption agreement between Qatar and Argentina allows Argentine citizens visa-free entry for tourism and business.
    Official source ↗
  • Romania✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-exempt under EU Regulation 1806/2018; 90-day stay permitted within any 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Saint Lucia✓
    Visa-free · 42 days
    Argentina nationals do not require a visa; permitted 6-week (42-day) visa-free stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Saint Vincent and the Grenadines✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens receive a visitor permit on arrival for 3 months with no pre-entry visa required.
    Official source ↗
  • San Marino✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens have visa-free access to San Marino for tourism with a standard 90-day short-stay limit.
    Official source ↗
  • Serbia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens may enter visa-free for up to 90 days within a 6-month period; return flights and sufficient funds required.
    Official source ↗
  • Seychelles✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa-free entry; visitor permit granted for up to 90 days upon arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Singapore✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens can enter Singapore visa-free for tourism with a valid passport (6+ months validity).
    Official source ↗
  • Slovakia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens do not need a visa to enter Slovakia (Schengen member) for stays up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Slovenia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens do not need a short-stay visa; visa-free entry allowed for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • South Africa✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine passport holders are visa-exempt for up to 90 days for tourism or business; yellow fever vaccination certificate required.
    Official source ↗
  • South Korea✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-exempt and may stay for up to 90 days for tourism purposes.
    Official source ↗
  • Spain✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are exempt from visa requirement for stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period under Regulation (EU) 2018/1806, Annex II.
    Official source ↗
  • Suriname✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    No visa required; must pay USD 50 entry fee online via VFS Global before arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Swaziland✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina citizens are visa-free for up to 30 days; passport must be valid for 3+ months with blank page.
    Official source ↗
  • Sweden✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens do not need a Schengen visa; visa-free entry permitted for up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Switzerland✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine nationals can stay visa-free in Switzerland for up to 90 days within any 180-day period as part of Schengen Area access.
    Official source ↗
  • Tajikistan✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens are included in Tajikistan's unilateral visa-free entry system for short-stay tourism (up to 30 days).
    Official source ↗
  • Thailand✓
    Visa-free · 60 days
    Argentina nationals receive 60-day visa-exempt entry to Thailand for tourism; extendable to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Tonga✓
    Visa-free · 31 days
    Visa-free entry for stays up to 31 days; passport valid 6+ months and onward ticket required.
    Official source ↗
  • Trinidad and Tobago✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens are visa-free for up to 90 days; passport must be valid for at least 6 months.
    Official source ↗
  • Tunisia
    Visa-free · 90 days
  • Turkey✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina passport holders are exempt from visa for tourist stays up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Ukraine✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine passport holders can enter Ukraine visa-free for up to 90 days within a 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • United Arab Emirates✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens enjoy visa-free entry for up to 90 days with a valid passport (must be valid for 6+ months).
    Official source ↗
  • Uruguay✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-free for tourism under Mercosur agreements and can stay up to 90 days; extension possible.
    Official source ↗
  • Uzbekistan✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina citizens are visa-free for up to 30 days; passport must have 6 months validity.
    Official source ↗
  • Vanuatu✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens are visa-exempt for tourist stays up to 30 days; longer stays require immigration office extension.
    Official source ↗
  • Vatican✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens are visa-free in the Schengen Area; Vatican maintains open borders and de facto follows Italian visa policy with no independent border control.
    Official source ↗
  • Venezuela✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can enter Venezuela without a visa for up to 90 days for tourism; extension possible for an additional 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Zambia✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Visa-free entry for Argentina nationals as of January 1, 2025; initial 30 days extendable to 90 days per 12-month period.
    Official source ↗

Visa on arrival

Visa issued at the border — typically quick, fee-paid, and no embassy visit required. Still bring passport photos, proof of onward travel, and hotel bookings.

  • Bahrain✓
    Visa-free · 14 days
    Argentina passport holders are eligible for visa on arrival at Bahrain International Airport with up to 14 days stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Cape Verde✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa on arrival available; 90-day stay extendable for same period. Pre-register online up to 5 days prior and pay Airport Security Fee (TSA).
    Official source ↗
  • Comoros✓
    Visa-free · 45 days
    Visa on arrival available at entry points for €60, valid for 45-day tourist stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Ghana✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Visa on arrival available; standard tourist stay of 30 days, extendable to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Guinea-Bissau✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina is not visa-exempt. Argentine citizens can obtain a visa on arrival or apply in advance at a Bissau-Guinean embassy, with a maximum stay of 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Iran
    Visa on arrival
  • Jordan
    Visa on arrival
  • Lebanon✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    One-month visa on arrival, extendable to three months total; cost-free for Argentine tourists.
    Official source ↗
  • Maldives✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Free 30-day tourist visa granted on arrival; no pre-approval required. Must complete Traveller Declaration via IMUGA within 96 hours before arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Marshall Islands✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa on arrival free for 90 days; Argentina has diplomatic relations with Marshall Islands.
    Official source ↗
  • Namibia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina passport holders eligible for visa on arrival; up to 90 days permitted for tourist stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Nepal✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa required; visa on arrival available at Tribhuvan International Airport in Kathmandu for 15/30/90-day tourist visas.
    Official source ↗
  • Palau✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Visa on arrival for 30 days; valid passport, return ticket, sufficient funds ($200 USD/week), Palau Entry Form QR code, and environmental pledge required.
    Official source ↗
  • Rwanda✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens can obtain a visa on arrival at Rwandan borders; valid for 30 days for single entry.
    Official source ↗
  • Samoa✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visitors receive a 90-day Visitor Permit on arrival; no pre-arranged visa required.
    Official source ↗
  • Senegal✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens can obtain a visa on arrival at Dakar Airport for stays up to 1 month.
    Official source ↗
  • Solomon Islands✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Free visitor permit on arrival for 30 days (up to 90 days aggregate per 12-month period); requires valid 6-month passport, onward ticket, and proof of funds.
    Official source ↗
  • Tanzania✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina nationals can obtain an Ordinary Visa (50 USD) on arrival or apply online; valid up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Timor-Leste✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Visa-on-arrival available at Dili International Airport for 30 days; extensible once for additional 30 days at $30 USD.
    Official source ↗
  • Tuvalu✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Visitor permit issued on arrival for AUD $100, valid up to one month. Passport valid 6+ months required.
    Official source ↗
  • Zimbabwe✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens can obtain a tourist visa upon arrival at Zimbabwe's port of entry or pre-apply online; valid for 30 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Israel✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine nationals require ETA-IL (electronic travel authorization) approved before travel; maximum 90 days per visit.
    Official source ↗
  • Kenya✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine passport holders must obtain an Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) online before travel; single-entry eTA allows up to 90 days stay.
    Official source ↗
  • New Zealand✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens qualify for visa waiver and require NZeTA (electronic travel authority); valid 2 years with up to 3 months per stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Palestine✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine tourists need ETA-IL electronic travel authorization (visa-exempt but ETA-IL required since January 2025); maximum 90-day stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Russia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens can enter Russia visa-free for 90 days within 180 days but must register through RuID electronic travel authorization system at least 72 hours before arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Saint Kitts and Nevis✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can obtain an eTA for 90 days; advance authorization required before travel.
    Official source ↗
  • United Kingdom✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    Argentine citizens require UK ETA (Electronic Travel Authorization) to visit; valid for 2 years and allows stays up to 6 months.
    Official source ↗

e-Visa destinations

Apply online before you travel. Usually approved within a few days, cheaper and faster than an embassy visa.

  • Afghanistan✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Tourist e-visa available; 30-day stay from date of entry, 90-day validity from issue. Cost $80 USD.
    Official source ↗
  • Albania✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens must apply for a Type C e-visa through https://e-visa.al/ for 90 days within a 180-day period.
    Official source ↗
  • Australia✓
    Visa-free · 365 days
    Argentine citizens must apply for a Visitor visa (subclass 600) online through ImmiAccount before travel; up to 12 months stay permitted.
    Official source ↗
  • Azerbaijan✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina citizens can apply for a single-entry e-visa via ASAN Visa system; valid for 30 days stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Benin✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens require an e-Visa for Benin tourism; 30-day single-entry or 90-day multiple-entry visas available via official portal.
    Official source ↗
  • Bhutan✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina passport holders must apply for e-Visa online through the Tourism Council of Bhutan; USD 40 visa fee plus USD 100/day Sustainable Development Fee required.
    Official source ↗
  • Burkina Faso✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Short-term tourist visa (eVisa) available online or on arrival; valid up to 90 days maximum.
    Official source ↗
  • Cambodia✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Tourist e-visa available online at evisa.gov.kh or visa on arrival at major airports and land borders; valid for one month.
    Official source ↗
  • Cameroon✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    E-visa required for tourism; obtainable online, collected at diplomatic mission or on arrival; Yellow Fever vaccination certificate mandatory.
    Official source ↗
  • Central African Republic✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    E-visa required for tourists; apply online, valid for 30 days, cannot be extended.
    Official source ↗
  • Chad✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can apply online for Chad's e-visa (€100, 90-day multiple-entry) via eVisa.td platform.
    Official source ↗
  • Congo✓
    Visa-free · 7 days
    Argentine citizens must apply for an online eVisa (Ordinary Flying Visa) for tourism; valid 90 days single entry, maximum 7-day stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Cuba✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens must obtain an e-visa online before travel; single entry valid for up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Djibouti✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens are eligible for a 30-day tourist e-visa, applied online or upon arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • DR Congo✓
    Visa-free · 7 days
    Argentine citizens can apply for a DRC eVisa online, valid for 90 days with a single entry and up to 7 days stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Egypt✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina passport holders are eligible for Egypt's single-entry tourist e-visa, valid for up to 30 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Equatorial Guinea✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens require an e-visa applied online before travel; short-term visas valid for maximum 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Ethiopia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens must apply online for tourist e-visa before arrival; valid for 30 or 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Gabon✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine G20 nationals must apply for e-Visa online before travel; single entry visa valid for 30 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Guinea✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine nationals require an e-visa; apply online with passport copy, vaccination proof, and travel documents; approval issued as Entry Visa Letter, visa completed at arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • India✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens can apply for Indian e-Tourist visa online; must apply minimum 4 days before travel.
    Official source ↗
  • Indonesia✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    e-Visa on Arrival (e-VOA) required; single-entry, 30-day permit valid for 90 days from issue.
    Official source ↗
  • Iraq✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina passport holders must apply for e-Visa through the official Iraqi e-Visa Portal before arrival; tourist visa allows 30-day stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Ivory Coast✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens must apply online via the official e-Visa portal (snedai.com/e-visa) at least 48 hours before travel; valid for 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Kuwait✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina is eligible for Kuwait eVisa (electronic visa application); valid for 30 days from issuance, allowing a single entry and stay up to 90 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Laos✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens can apply for eVisa online (valid 60 days from issue, 30-day max stay) or obtain Visa on Arrival at entry points.
    Official source ↗
  • Libya✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Tourist e-visa required; single entry, 90-day validity, max 30-day stay; invitation letter and tourism police escort mandatory.
    Official source ↗
  • Madagascar✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Tourist e-visa available online through Madagascar's official portal; visa-on-arrival also obtainable at airport.
    Official source ↗
  • Malawi✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens are eligible for e-Visa; visa on arrival also available at selected entry points.
    Official source ↗
  • Mali✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens must apply online for a tourist e-visa through Mali's official Ministry of Foreign Affairs portal.
    Official source ↗
  • Mauritania✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens require an e-visa; standard tourist visa is 30 days (90-day option also available).
    Official source ↗
  • Mozambique✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine tourists must apply for an eVisa online; visa exemption not available. eVisa valid for 30, 60, or 90 days depending on option selected.
    Official source ↗
  • Myanmar✓
    Visa-free · 28 days
    E-visa (online) required for Argentine tourists; valid 90 days from issue, permits 28-day stay; $50 USD; 3 working days processing.
    Official source ↗
  • Nigeria✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens require an electronic visa (eVisa) for tourism; apply online via Nigerian Immigration Service portal.
    Official source ↗
  • Pakistan✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentina citizens eligible for Pakistan tourist e-visa valid for 90 days; apply online before travel.
    Official source ↗
  • Papua New Guinea✓
    Visa-free · 60 days
    Argentine citizens must apply for an online e-visa through Papua New Guinea's Immigration & Citizenship Authority (evisa.ica.gov.pg) before travel; valid for 60 days.
    Official source ↗
  • Sao Tome and Principe✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens must apply for an e-Visa online through the eVisaST system; not listed among visa-exempt countries.
    Official source ↗
  • Sierra Leone✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine nationals (non-ECOWAS) must apply online for an e-visa before travel; allows maximum 30-day stay within 90-day validity.
    Official source ↗
  • Somalia✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens must apply for an eVisa at evisa.gov.so before travel; valid for up to 30 days.
    Official source ↗
  • South Sudan✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens can apply for a single-entry e-visa online; valid for up to 90 days from date of entry.
    Official source ↗
  • Sri Lanka✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens require an ETA (e-visa) for tourist visits; valid 30 days from arrival, double entry allowed.
    Official source ↗
  • Togo✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens must apply for an e-visa before travel. Togo no longer issues visas on arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Uganda✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens must apply for and obtain an e-visa online before arrival; valid for up to 90 days for tourism.
    Official source ↗
  • Vietnam✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentina passport holders eligible for 30-day single-entry e-visa via Vietnamese Immigration Department electronic system.
    Official source ↗

Visa required — 20 destinations

embassy visa needed · click to expand

These destinations require a full visa from an embassy or consulate in advance. Entries marked ✓ have been re-verified against an official government source.

  • Algeria✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Tourist visa required in advance; must include certificate of accommodation, hotel reservation, or travel agency certificate.
    Official source ↗
  • Bangladesh✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Visa required; apply at Bangladesh embassy/consulate. Argentina not on visa-on-arrival list.
    Official source ↗
  • Brunei✓
    Visa required
    Argentine citizens (Category B) require a visa applied through the nearest Brunei mission abroad before travel.
    Official source ↗
  • Burundi✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Multiple-entry tourist visa required; apply through embassy in advance for $90 visa valid 30 days (renewable to 90 days total).
    Official source ↗
  • Canada✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    Visa required; exception: Argentine citizens with Canadian visa in last 10 years or valid US visa can use eTA instead.
    Official source ↗
  • Eritrea✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens require a visa issued in advance by the Eritrean Embassy; processing takes minimum 15 business days; tourist visa typically valid for one month after arrival.
    Official source ↗
  • Gambia✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Argentine citizens must obtain a tourist visa in advance from a Gambian diplomatic mission; visa is issued for single entry within 3 months with maximum 30-day stay.
    Official source ↗
  • Kosovo✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Type C visa required, applied in person at Kosovo embassy/consulate; valid for max 90 days within 180 days; €40 fee.
    Official source ↗
  • Lesotho✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Tourist visa required; apply to Ministry of Home Affairs via visa-applications.immigration@gov.ls at least 5 working days before travel.
    Official source ↗
  • Liberia✓
    Visa-free · 14 days
    Tourist visa required; Argentine citizens must apply at Liberian Consulate General in Buenos Aires (not via portal). Valid 14 days from issuance.
    Official source ↗
  • Nauru✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens must obtain a visitor visa in advance through Nauru Immigration Division; no visa on arrival available.
    Official source ↗
  • Niger✓
    Visa-free · 30 days
    Visa required. Argentina not on visa-exempt list. E-visa available for single entry valid up to 30 days.
    Official source ↗
  • North Korea✓
    Visa required
    Visa required; must be arranged via licensed travel agency for organized tour only. Issued as separate tourist card.
    Official source ↗
  • Saudi Arabia✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Tourist visa required; Argentine citizens not eligible for e-visa and must apply at Saudi embassy or consulate.
    Official source ↗
  • Sudan✓
    Visa required
    Argentine nationals must obtain a visa from a Sudanese embassy; not eligible for eVisa (limited to specific countries).
    Official source ↗
  • Syria✓
    Visa required
    Tourist visa required; apply in advance at Syrian embassy with passport (valid 6+ months), photos, and proof of accommodation and travel plans. Processing ~7-10 business days.
    Official source ↗
  • Taiwan✓
    Visa-free · 90 days
    Argentine citizens require a 90-day visitor visa, obtainable through the Taipei Commercial and Cultural Office in Argentina (visaocct@gmail.com).
    Official source ↗
  • Turkmenistan✓
    Visa-free · 10 days
    Visa required; must obtain letter of invitation from licensed Turkmenistan travel agency; standard tourist visa valid up to 10 days single entry.
    Official source ↗
  • United States✓
    Visa-free · 180 days
    B-2 tourist visa required; applies at US Embassy in Buenos Aires; CBP determines authorized stay at port of entry, typically up to 6 months.
    Official source ↗
  • Yemen✓
    Visa required
    Argentine citizens must obtain a visa in advance from a Yemeni diplomatic mission; not eligible for visa-free or visa-on-arrival entry.
    Official source ↗

Expand your access with a residence permit

If your Argentine passport has limited reach, a residence permit can unlock many additional destinations. Pairing your passport with one of the permits below typically adds 5–15 new visa-free or visa-on-arrival countries:

  • UAE residence permit — strong for South Asian passport holders, unlocks Armenia, Georgia, Azerbaijan, Serbia
  • US Green Card — unlocks Mexico, Canada (via eTA), several Caribbean and Central American countries
  • Schengen residence permit — unlocks Turkey, Balkans, several visa-free short stays across the EU

Frequently asked questions

How many countries can Argentine passport holders visit visa-free?
Argentine passport holders have visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to approximately 134 countries — 106 visa-free and 28 visa-on-arrival. Notable visa-free destinations include Andorra, Angola, Antigua and Barbuda, Armenia, Austria.
Is the Argentine passport strong?
Yes — the Argentine passport is considered strong, giving access to 134+ destinations without needing to arrange a visa in advance.
Do I need an ETA or e-visa for some countries?
Yes. 44 destinations require an e-visa (online application before arrival) for Argentine passport holders. These are typically quicker than embassy visas but still need to be arranged before travel.
Which countries require a full visa in advance?
20 countries require Argentine passport holders to obtain a visa from an embassy before travelling. Check the destination embassy's website for specific requirements and processing times.
Can a residence permit or second visa unlock extra countries?
Yes — several residence permits grant bonus visa-free access regardless of your Argentine passport. See our guides for UAE residence, US Green Card, and Schengen residence permit to learn which countries unlock for each.
How accurate is this list?
Entries marked with a ✓ badge have been re-verified against an official government source (embassy, ministry, or visa portal) and link to that source. Entries without the badge come from the community-maintained open-source Passport Index Dataset — a reasonable starting point but not individually verified. Rules change frequently. Always confirm with the destination embassy before booking travel.

  • Indian passport
  • British passport
  • American passport
  • Canadian passport
